text.title: Junior AI-Overreliance Coaching Guide Examples
text.summary: Worked example: coaching a junior who shipped axios + undefended IOException in a Java codebase.

example.context: Junior (8 months) submitted Java PR adding HTTP client for a new feature. Codebase uses `JdkHttpClient` everywhere. Junior chose `axios` (which doesn't exist for Java — AI hallucinated a port). Line 42 catches IOException with `e.printStackTrace();`.
example.walk: Step 1: signals = library-of-the-day (axios not in pom.xml), undefended-exception (printStackTrace), style-mix (camelCase mixed with snake_case in same class). Step 2: 3 signals → block. Step 3: comment: 'Nice job covering the happy path on the parser. Three flags: (1) `axios` doesn't ship for Java — were you targeting the JS port? our convention is `JdkHttpClient` (see services/payment/). (2) Line 42 prints stack trace and continues — what should happen if the upstream HTTP call fails? (3) `getUserId()` and `get_address()` mix styles in one class; pick one.' Step 4: 'Walk me through how you'd write lines 38-46 without the AI suggestion — what would be your first instinct?' Step 5: PR blocked; pairing scheduled.
example.output: Junior responds with rewrite using JdkHttpClient + retry policy + structured error log; learns three patterns in one cycle.
